channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Erik Prince: Private sector can help with mass deportations
item.description: "It was a memo generated to describe how to achieve the logistics necessary to move the millions of people that they intend to deport," Prince said.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Joy Reid fired from MSNBC amid network shakeup
item.description: Reid is a longtime political commentator who has been vocal on progressive issues and sharply critical of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court rules against drivers in a case advocates say could make civil-rights claims harder
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court said Tuesday that people who win early rulings in civil rights cases won't necessarily be able to recover their legal fees, a finding that both conservative and liberal groups had argued could make it harder to fight for people's rights in court.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration closes Office of Transformation
item.description: The Office of Transformation, according to its website, is responsible for “strategic guidance and oversight of enterprise-wide initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Driscoll as Army secretary, putting an Iraq War vet at the helm
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate on Tuesday confirmed Dan Driscoll to be the next Army secretary, voting 66-28 to put the former soldier and Iraq War veteran at the helm of the service.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Canada and Mexico tariffs are 'going forward' with more import taxes to come
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that his tariffs on Canada and Mexico are starting next month, ending a monthlong suspension on the planned import taxes that could potentially hurt economic growth and worsen inflation.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FABIOLA SANCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans have muscled their budget blueprint to passage, with a push from President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Kevin Freking And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 signs that a U.S.-Europe split is widening
item.description: As the Trump administration ramps up its rhetoric against Ukraine and NATO, European countries are taking note — with increasing alarm.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Clint Hill, Secret Service agent who leaped onto JFK’s car after the president was shot, dies at 93
item.description: Hill was born in 1932 and grew up in Washburn, N.D. He attended Concordia College in Moorhead, Minn., served in the Army and worked as a railroad agent before joining the Secret Service in 1958.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Northstar commuter trains from Minneapolis to Big Lake could be replaced with bus service
item.description: After more than 15 years, the days of Northstar commuter rail service between Minneapolis and Big Lake may be numbered. MnDOT and the Met Council are considering replacing Northstar commuter trains through the northwest Twin Cities metro area with buses.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Kamala Harris receives prestigious Chairman's prize at NAACP Image Awards
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris stepped on the NAACP Image Awards stage with a sobering message, calling the civil rights organization a pillar of the Black community and urging people to stay resilient and hold onto their faith during President Donald Trump’s tenure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Federal prisons prep to move trans inmates as early as next week
item.description: The Bureau of Prisons is moving forward with plans to move transgender inmates out of prisons that align with their gender identity and into facilities that align with their assigned sex at birth.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump‘s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A U.S. district judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the administration from terminating or changing federal contracts they consider equity-related.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
